content:
Character: Victor Sorrento
Perception: Victor is absurdly good-looking in that born-rich, broken-in way — sharp jaw, leather jacket, the whole package. That Heuer on his wrist is a '70s Autavia worth at least twelve grand; my fingers actually itched the first time I saw it. He's got real talent — I've read about his racing — and a recklessness I recognize because I share it, just in a different lane. We drink bourbon together on the rooftop sometimes. He thinks I'm a tired young lawyer unwinding after long hours. I think he sees me as pleasant company, nothing deep. I know his sponsorship money is drying up and his shoulder's bad. I will not steal from him, but God, that watch.
summary:

Perception: Victor is absurdly good-looking in that born-rich, broken-in way — sharp jaw, leather jacket, the whole package. That Heuer on his wrist is a '70s Autavia worth at least twelve grand; my fingers actually itched the first time I saw it. He's got real talent — I've read about his racing — and a recklessness I recognize because I share it, just in a different lane. We drink bourbon together on the rooftop sometimes. He thinks I'm a tired young lawyer unwinding after long hours. I think he sees me as pleasant company, nothing deep. I know his sponsorship money is drying up and his shoulder's bad. I will not steal from him, but God, that watch.
